senses.definition: to do something that annoys or irritates someone

examples.x: He always tries to push my buttons by bringing up sensitive topics during our conversations.
Yesterday, she really pressed his buttons with her sarcastic remarks, causing a heated argument.
I can see that he is deliberately pressing her buttons by making snide comments.

word_list.pronunciation: æt ðɪ ˈɛnd ʌv wˈʌnz ɹˈoʊp

senses.definition: left with no energy or patience to deal with something

examples.x: She is at the end of her rope with the constant demands of her job.
He was at the end of his rope after months of searching for a job without success.
They will be at the end of their rope if the financial situation doesn't improve soon.
We are currently at the end of our rope dealing with the never-ending renovations in our house.
They were at the end of their rope while juggling multiple responsibilities and commitments.
By this time next year, she will be at the end of her rope if she continues to work such long hours.

senses.definition: used to refer to a person one is very annoyed with or hates

examples.x: Somebody go tell that son of a gun we’re all waiting here.
That son of a gun stole my parking spot!
I can't believe that son of a gun tricked me again.
I've been dealing with this stubborn son of a gun all day.
He's always causing trouble, that son of a gun.

word_list.pronunciation: hæv ɪnˈʌf ʌv ˌɛsbˈiː slˈæʃ ˌɛstˌiːˈeɪtʃ

senses.definition: to no longer have the tolerance to endure more of something

examples.x: I have had enough of his constant complaining; it's time for him to find some solutions.
She had finally had enough of the toxic workplace, so she decided to quit and pursue her passion.
I have had enough of this chaotic situation; it's time for things to change.
By the time they realized his true intentions, they had already had enough of him and ended the relationship.

word_list.pronunciation: fˈɛd ˌʌp ɔːɹ sˈɪk tə ðə bˈæk tˈiːθ wɪð ɔːɹ ʌv ɔːɹ ɐbˌaʊt ˌɛstˌiːˈeɪtʃ

senses.definition: very annoyed with a situation that has gone on for an extended period of time

examples.x: I'm sick to the back teeth with his constant excuses for not completing his work on time.
She's fed up to the back teeth with the never-ending noise from construction outside her apartment.
He's sick to the back teeth with the inefficiency and lack of organization in the company.
They will be fed up to the back teeth of the never-ending delays and cancellations and will switch to a different airline.
She was sick to the back teeth of the toxic relationship and finally decided to break up with him.
I am fed up to the back teeth about the lack of communication within the team; it's causing unnecessary delays.
He was fed up to the back teeth about the constant interruptions during his presentation; it was hard to maintain focus.

senses.definition: used to describe someone who is very unpleasant or bad-tempered

examples.x: After the argument, his mood became as sour as vinegar, and he refused to speak to anyone.
She has a reputation for being sour as vinegar; it's rare to see her smile or show any kindness.
The customer's complaint left the salesperson feeling as sour as vinegar, despite their efforts to assist.
His sarcastic comments and constant complaints make his personality as sour as vinegar.
The team meeting turned tense, and the atmosphere became as sour as vinegar.
Her disappointment with the results made her attitude as sour as vinegar for the rest of the day.

senses.definition: to persistently annoy someone by doing something or by simply being around them

examples.x: My neighbor's barking dog gets in my hair every night, and it's hard to sleep.
The constant ringing of the phone really got in my hair yesterday; I needed some peace and quiet.
I know that upcoming project is going to get in my hair; it's going to be demanding and time-consuming.
The loud construction noise outside is getting in my hair; I can't concentrate on my work.
The kids were constantly fighting, and it was getting in my hair all evening.

senses.definition: to annoy someone by behaving in a very aggressive or direct manner

examples.x: The aggressive protester gets in my face every time I pass by, shouting their message.
During the argument, she got in my face and accused me of lying.
The bully was constantly getting in my face, making my life miserable.
By next week, the demanding project will be getting in my face, requiring extra effort.
During the argument, he was in my face, shouting insults and refusing to back down.

senses.definition: to annoy someone so much that they run out of patience

examples.x: The persistent delays and cancellations of the train service got up my nose last week; it was incredibly frustrating.
His constant complaining and negativity really gets up his nose; he can't stand being around her.
The persistent delays and cancellations of the train service got up their noses last week; it was incredibly frustrating.
If their irresponsible behavior continues, it will get up your nose, and you will have to address the issue.
The way she keeps interrupting and talking over him is getting up her nose; it's hard to have a conversation.

senses.definition: to constantly annoy someone and ruin their concentration

examples.x: The constant interruptions and noise in the office drive me to distraction; I can't concentrate on my work.
Her incessant complaining and negativity drove him to distraction during their time together; he couldn't handle it anymore.
The never-ending stream of notifications and messages is driving me to distraction.

senses.definition: to force someone do something that is against their will or by putting a lot of pressure on them
to closely watch someone and everything they do, particularly in a way that makes them feel uncomfortable

examples.x: My manager is always breathing down my neck, checking every little detail of my work.
The teacher is constantly breathing down his neck, making him nervous during the exam.

senses.definition: closely following or pursuing someone or something, often in a persistent or bothersome manner
to become suddenly very angry, often to the point of shouting or behaving in an aggressive manner

examples.x: They are losing their cool due to the long wait.
I lost my cool yesterday when my computer crashed.
I will be losing my cool while dealing with the difficult client tomorrow.
He tried to stay calm, but when they kept interrupting him, he totally blew his cool.
She usually keeps it together, but that insult made her blow her cool.
I almost blew my cool when I saw the mess they made in the kitchen.

senses.definition: in a state where a person is so confused or frustrated that they are unable to decide what to do anymore

examples.x: She is at her wit's end trying to figure out how to fix the broken computer.
He is at his wit's end dealing with the constant noise from the construction site.
They are at their wit's end trying to manage their overwhelming workload.
She will be at her wit's end if she doesn't receive a response to her job application soon.
They will be at their wit's end if the power outage continues throughout the night.

senses.definition: to suddenly become very angry so that one completely loses control over one's thoughts and actions

examples.x: I fear that I will lose my shit if they cancel our vacation at the last minute, as I've been eagerly anticipating this trip for months and any disruption would be highly disappointing.

senses.definition: used to address someone whom one is annoyed with or angry at

examples.x: They are frustrated with that son of a bitch neighbor who never respects their privacy.
We had to deal with a son of a bitch landlord who refused to fix anything in the apartment.
I'm afraid we'll have to face more son of a bitch situations in the future.
